A.name: Model Collapse Theory
A.description: Recursive training degradation, variance growth, tail collapse, MAD, self-consuming models. Motivation-only evidence after v2, not a primary post-training proof line.

B.name: Web Data Pollution & Scale
B.description: AI-generated content fraction on web, CommonCrawl contamination estimates, bot traffic, synthetic text prevalence. Supports background motivation around contamination pressure.

C.name: Detection & Reactive Limits
C.description: AI text detection, watermarking, detector limitations. Compressed category used to show why reactive defenses do not fully solve contamination risk.

D.name: Information Theory + Text Quality Metrics
D.description: n-gram entropy, perplexity, KL divergence, Renyi divergence, TTR, text-diversity measurement tools. Supports web-drift measurement and the L_auth framework.

E.name: Data Quality & Curation for Training
E.description: Data curation, deduplication, quality filtering, data mixing ratios, scaling laws, Phi textbook-quality approach. Supporting category for framework and pilot design, reduced after v2 reprioritization.

F.name: Human Data Value & RLHF
F.description: RLHF cost, preference data quality, human annotation vs synthetic labeling, human-in-the-loop, alignment tax. Core evidence for post-training provenance value.

G.name: Platform & Provenance Design
G.description: Data provenance verification, community platforms, anti-algorithmic design, append-only data systems. Grounds CampusGo as a deployed provenance-aware collection platform.

H.name: Temporal Web Quality Measurement
H.description: Corpus linguistics temporal analysis, CommonCrawl quality over time, web text entropy trends, longitudinal corpus studies. Supports motivation-only web-drift measurement.

I.name: Social Reasoning Benchmarks
I.description: SocialIQA, EmpatheticDialogues, social intelligence evaluation, social reasoning tasks, empathy benchmarks. Core evaluation substrate for post-training claims.

J.name: Fine-tune Data Composition Ablation
J.description: LoRA/QLoRA data composition studies, training-set quality vs quantity ablations, instruction-tuning mixture effects. Core methodology evidence for the primary line.

K.name: Alternative Mechanisms / Competing Explanations
K.description: Inference-time scaling, chain-of-thought, extended thinking, test-time compute, model-scale effects, and other mechanisms that may improve social reasoning without changing training-data composition.

_note: 11 categories (A-K). Every paper should map to one primary category plus optional secondary categories; Category K is required for competing explanations.
